arts.ttl: Arsenal XI vs Leicester – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Arsenal travel to Leicester City in the Premier League on Saturday lunchtime looking to cut Liverpool’s lead at the top. A draw in the Merseyside Derby opened up a seven-point gap between the leaders and Arsenal, who will aim to put the pressure on their title rivals this weekend. Kai Havertz's hamstring injury sustained during a training session means the Gunners are incredibly short in attack.

arts.ttl: Arsenal guaranteed to be without five players for Leicester as Ben White decision confirmed
arts.sum: Asked if White will return to the squad this weekend, Arteta said in his pre-match press conference: "Yeah, Ben has been training more regularly now so we have to still manage a lot his load. Arsenal's injury woes have been well documented in recent weeks but Mikel Arteta will finally welcome back one crucial player to the squad against Leicester City on Saturday.

arts.ttl: 'He can still play': Gary Neville says player Arsenal released for free would be 'perfect' for Man United now
Arsenal starlet who worked with Thierry Henry and Cristiano Ronaldo can derail Gunners’ title bid
arts.sum: Former Arsenal wonderkid Stephy Mavididi has taken a circuitous route ti the Premier League but the Leicester City star has a chance to deal his old employers a major blow

arts.ttl: Leicester City chairman addresses frustration and transfer business ahead of planned protest
arts.sum: Leicester City chairman Aiyawatt ‘Top’ Srivaddhanaprabha has acknowledged the frustration around the club and the need for improvements, but asked for the King Power Stadium crowd to get behind the team during Saturday’s match against Arsenal (12.30pm). “We know some of you are frustrated, we respect the views you have shared and we know there are things we need to improve together as one club,” Top writes in his programme notes.
